name: Stewed Rabbit
recipeIngredient: 2-3 lbs domestic or 2 wild Rabbits, cut up
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix flour, salt, and pepper. Coat the rabbit pieces with the flour mixture.
2. Cook the bacon until crisp; drain and crumble. Set aside.
3. In a Dutch oven, cook 2 tablespoons of bacon fat over medium heat. Add the rabbit and brown on all sides, turning occasionally.
4. Add onions, carrots, garlic, and bay leaf. Stir together.
5. Mix 1/4 cup water, dry red wine, packed brown sugar, salt, rosemary, and paprika in a separate bowl.
6. Pour the mixture over the rabbit, ensuring it is fully covered.
7. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and cover. Simmer until the rabbit is tender, about 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
8. Remove the bay leaf and set aside.
9. Mix cornstarch with 2 tablespoons cold water in a separate bowl.
10. In the Dutch oven, stir into the liquid until smooth.
11. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly, then boil for one minute.
12. Pour the sauce over the vegetables and rabbit.
